semanticCore: Striving, running, walking fast, or earning
quranicRelevance: Striving/exerting effort for faith (Sa'y - 'sa'yaha'), running/walking fast, and earning livelihood.

keyDerivations.meaning: To strive, run, walk quickly, earn, or exert effort
Course, path, or place of striving

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَأَنْ لَيْسَ لِلإِنْسَانِ إِلَّا مَا سَعَى (39:39): And that there is not for man except what he strived/earned.
فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ السَّعْيَ (37:102): Then when he reached (the age of) running/striving with him (maturity).
discardedNoise: The physical running of the Safa and Marwa ritual (Sa'y) - treated under spiritual application.
summaryEn: The root signifies active striving, outlining both physical running/exertion and the ultimate spiritual accountability of deeds (Sa'y).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على السعي والجد، واصفاً المشي السريع والنشاط المادي، والمسؤولية والمحاسبة الأخروية على الأعمال (سعي).
rawText: سعى 1 , aor. ﹷ; (S A, Msb, K;) and سَغَبَ, aor. ﹹ; (A, K;) inf. n. سَغَبٌ, (S, A, * Msb, K,) which is of the former, (Sudot;, Msb, TA,) and سَغْبٌ, (K,) which is of the latter, (TA,) and سُغُوبٌ (K) and سَغَابَةٌ and مَسْغَبَةٌ, (A, * K,) the last syn. with مَجَاعَةٌ; (S, Msb;) He was, or became, hungry: (S, A, Msb, K:) or suffered hunger together with fatigue. (A, Msb, K.) You say, بِهِ سَغَبٌ and مَسْغَبَةٌ and سَغَابَةٌ, [using these ns. as simple substs.,] In him is hunger: or hunger together with fatigue. (A.) And يَتِيمٌ ذُو مَسْغَبَةٍ [An orphan] having hunger. (S.) And فِى يَوْمٍ ذِى مَسْغَبَةٍ, in the Kur [xc. 14], means In a day of hunger. (TA.) [See also سَغْبَةٌ and سَغَبٌ below.]
